---
applyTo: "**/*"
description: "Documentation requirements for user-facing features. Always check this file when working on features that the user may interact with."
---

# Documentation Requirements

When adding or modifying user-facing features, update the user guide (`docs/user/`) in the same PR. The guide follows the Diátaxis framework with three sections:

## What to update

| Change type | Where to document |
|-------------|------------------|
| New TOML config field | Update the relevant `docs/user/reference/config/` page (e.g., `components.md`, `distros.md`) — add to field tables and include examples |
| New CLI command or flag | Fix the Cobra `Short`/`Long`/flag descriptions in Go source. CLI docs in `docs/user/reference/cli/` are **auto-generated** — never hand-edit them. Run `mage build` to regenerate. |
| New user workflow | Add or update a `docs/user/how-to/` page |
| Behavioral change | Update the relevant explanation or reference page |

## Auto-generated CLI docs

Files under `docs/user/reference/cli/` are generated by `azldev docs markdown` (invoked automatically by `mage build`). To update CLI documentation:

1. Edit the Cobra command/flag descriptions in Go source code
2. Run `mage build` — this rebuilds the binary and regenerates CLI docs
3. Commit the regenerated files

**Never hand-edit files under `docs/user/reference/cli/`** — they will be overwritten on the next build.
